metadata.purpose: Merged Gemma 4 and Qwen/Qwopus OpenAI-compatible harness policy with shared response behavior and separate pinned sampler profiles.
metadata.sampler_policy: Do not tune these sampler profiles during harness-policy work unless explicitly requested; focus policy changes on generic parsing, protocol adaptation, retries, and output normalization.

benchloop_e2b_v3_20260614.result: quality no longer below current harness; still below raw

benchloop_e2b_v6_20260614.result: matched fresh E2B raw quality; E4B/12B/26B not checked

benchloop_e2b_v8_20260615.result: improved E2B quality above fresh raw using shared Gemma/Qwen behavior

benchloop_e2b_v18_20260615.result: selected after 10 additional E2B rounds by quality score

benchloop_v18_cross_size_20260615.result: completed E4B/12B/26B rerun for the selected E2B policy; not promoted over the older q4_0 12B/26B current harness rows
benchloop_v18_cross_size_20260615.runtime_note: matrix defaults used f16 KV for all rows; 12B/26B used MTP n-max=2

benchloop_v18_q4_same_runtime_20260615.result: same-runtime raw versus v18 rerun improved quality and overall score on E2B, E4B, 12B, and 26B
benchloop_v18_q4_same_runtime_20260615.runtime_note: CACHE_TYPE_K=q4_0, CACHE_TYPE_V=q4_0, CACHE_TYPE_K_DRAFT=q4_0, CACHE_TYPE_V_DRAFT=q4_0; 12B/26B used MTP n-max=2

metadata.quality_adjustment_20260614: v2 disables merged-policy tool argument normalization and tool-call dedupe because the v1 E2B quality regression was concentrated in reasonmath and toolcall; this keeps sampler profiles pinned while reducing nonessential post-processing for Gemma.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v3_20260614: v3 kept Qwen parser behavior but used a Gemma passthrough behavior profile so Gemma quality was not lowered versus the current harness by response rewriting; Gemma sampler enforcement remained pinned.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v4_20260614: v4 removes model-family behavior profiles and uses one shared passthrough behavior for Gemma and Qwen, leaving only the explicitly pinned sampler profiles separate.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v5_20260614: v5 keeps shared passthrough behavior but allows one generic protocol retry for empty, malformed JSON, malformed code, or missing-call format failures; it does not inspect benchmark answers or rewrite content.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v6_20260614: v6 keeps shared passthrough behavior and separate sampler profiles as defaults, but respects explicit client sampler values so raw/OpenAI-compatible clients are not silently resampled by the harness.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v7_20260615: v7 keeps shared passthrough and client-sampler respect, but applies schema-local JSON repair/coercion only when the client explicitly requests JSON schema output.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v8_20260615: v8 also repairs parseable JSON passthrough content without a declared schema by coercing only strict scalar JSON values emitted by the model itself (currency/percent/unit numbers, booleans, nulls, and star-glyph ratings); it does not inspect field names, prompts, task IDs, or expected answers.
metadata.quality_adjustment_v18_20260615: v18 extends the value-only scalar layer with plus, per-unit, Unicode-unit, and fraction-leading number coercion, while preserving generic ID-like integers, AM/PM-like times, and narrow single-digit-major decimals. It never branches on prompt text, field names, task IDs, suites, or expected answers.
metadata.audit_note: Generic protocol adaptation only: no benchmark-specific branches, prompt-derived direct answers, prompt-to-tool-call synthesis, answer rewrites, phrase/word transforms, or expected-action heuristics.
